265 housing units at Beacon Station on Metro-North's Hudson Line, including one-to-one replacement commuter parking
Contingency Recovery Rides for Access-A-Ride customers, extended until June 2023

Four procurement items for an estimated expenditure of $11.4 million, including contract modifications with Arrow Inc., LEAP, and LimoSys (E-Hail Pilot Program brokers) and RFP for nine R259 10-ton crane cars for NYCT
New yard support facility completion March 2020, Yard and supporting infrastructure completion October 2020, Closeout forecast July 2023

records.status: expanded beyond GCT to North White Plains
records.description: Expanded Demand Response Program beyond Grand Central Terminal to include the North White Plains office complex. Load-management strategies lower peak-period energy demand and support regional grid reliability.

Design-Build services to upgrade hardware and software for Metro-North Railroad's Private Branch Exchange (PBX) system. Will integrate a recording system to locally record audio communications at MNR's OCC, back-up OCC, and key facilities across Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines.

records.project_name: Together Railroads and Communities Keeping Safe (TRACKS)
records.description: Program providing opportunities for MNR to educate riders and the public on safe behaviors when traveling on trains and navigating railroad grade crossings. Strengthens public awareness and reinforces safe practices.

records.project_name: Refinancing of ESD TIFIA Loan for Moynihan Station Development Project Phase 2
records.description: To obtain MTA Board approval to enter into various agreements necessary to support the refinancing of an existing TIFIA loan up to $633 million for Phase 2 of the Moynihan Station Development Project
MTA 20-Year Needs Assessment covering the period 2025-2044, focusing on three challenges: Increasing Resiliency, Aging Infrastructure, and Changing Rider Needs

Implementation of MTA Automated Vehicle Location Monitoring System for all MTA Agencies. Includes delivery and installation of all hardware, cloud-based software, wireless data service, maintenance and support services in the not-to-exceed amount of $4,026,393. Replaces current AVLM system for non-passenger fleet of approximately 2,500 vehicles.
MTA committed to saving $500 million in recurring operating costs, starting with $100 million 2023, $400 million in 2024, growing to $500 million in 2025

records.project_name: Small Business Development and Mentoring Program (SBDMP) Contract
records.description: Competitively negotiated contract for management of MTA's Small Business Development and Mentoring Program, including mentoring services, bank loan repayment administration, payment tracking, and project meeting attendance

records.project_name: Multiple Series Refunding Bond Supplemental Resolutions
records.description: Approved by the Board in December 2023, authorizing 2024 refundings to which the updated Refunding Policy will apply
Paratransit initiative that enables customers to receive authorization for taxis directly from the MYMTA Web/APP

records.project_name: North White Plains Station Parking License Agreement
records.description: Five-year license (May 1, 2022-April 30, 2027) for County of Westchester to operate and maintain Metro-North's 392-space commuter parking lot at North White Plains Station. 60/40% gross revenue share. County covers maintenance from its 60% share.

records.project_name: Project Management Consultant Services for the New York Customer Service Center Contracts
records.description: Personal service contract for Project Management Consultant Services for the New York Customer Service Center Contracts
16 additional initiatives detailed in financial plan
Four simulators for Operator training and retraining; one at Spring Creek Depot and three at Zerega.
Adjust scheduled maintenance system cycle program for cars while maintaining reliability
Comprehensive effort including recognition for exemplary attendance; expanded employee engagement; and joint working groups with Labor to reduce assaults
Including partnering with third-party administrator to improve care while reducing costs
Lower costs and greenhouse gas emissions through efforts including LED lighting and optimizing heating and cooling (e.g., remote boiler monitoring)
Eliminated contract cleaning and hired additional cleaners for stations and cars
Modification to add funding to cover the Retroactive Extension Period
Range of strategies including enforcement of existing timekeeping rules and reduction of overtime in targeted right areas
Bring specifications for material purchases in line with business needs
Expand role in customer service and increase hourly pay, while reducing net costs by optimizing coverage requirements (e.g., during lunch breaks)

Vision Zero provides training on defensive driving practices for Bus Operators. Training is provided once every two years and rotated with DMV Article 19A training. The update is in concept phase with updated topics directed at Bus Operators. Rollout starting in July.
Not-to-exceed budget of $385.1M to finalize OCIP procurement for September 30, 2023 - December 31, 2025, covering 229 projects with total construction value of $8.5 billion

records.project_name: Open Trip Planner (OTP) Hosting, Maintenance, and Support
records.description: Three-year extension of service agreement with Cambridge Systematics for continued hosting, maintenance, and support of the Open Trip Planner application including upgrade to OTP 2.0, mobility data hub expansion, and integration with other MTA applications
New enterprise technology system for Paratransit Access-A-Ride operations, replacing existing RTS/CAD and AVLM systems

Metro-North partnered with the Building and Construction Trades Council of Greater New York and the Apprenticeship Readiness Collective to host a job fair at Tito Puente Educational Complex in East Harlem for the Park Avenue Viaduct project. Connected members of the East Harlem community with project-related construction jobs.
Partial building demolition of the LIRR section of the building located at 87-46 123rd Street, Richmond Hill, New York

records.project_name: Pearl River Station Building Lease - Bru Brew LLC
records.description: Revised lease terms with Bru Brew LLC for operation of a restaurant at Pearl River Station. Rent escalation changed from 3% to 2% annually. Two extension terms of five years each at fair market value. Updated NPV: $349,670.

records.project_name: Penn Station LIRR Train Hall Renovation Project
records.description: Extension of project management consultant services contract with WSP USA, Inc. for an additional 12 month period from January 23, 2023 to January 22, 2024 due to alignment with construction project schedule
Design services for Penn Station reconstruction
Upgrade to MTA's PeopleSoft ERP System including People data, digital transformation, and functional enhancements. Part of the MTA Transformation Plan approved by the MTA Board in June 2019.

Options for Yard Track extensions are severely limited due to extensive development near the station. Advancement contingent upon MTA LIRR reaching an agreement with the Town of North Hempstead for real estate acquisition/swaps.

Development of 80,617sf industrial property on East River waterfront in Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Conditional designation of Gotham Property Acquisition LLC to redevelop via 99-year ground lease. NYCT Mobile Wash and Material Storage Units to relocate.

records.project_name: Quest Diagnostics Drug & Alcohol Testing Services Contract
records.description: Award of a competitively negotiated miscellaneous service contract to Quest Diagnostics Clinical Laboratories Inc. to provide Drug & Alcohol Testing Services in support of MTA pre- and post-hire workplace drug/alcohol testing policies for the MTA agencies. Contract term April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2030, not-to-exceed amount of $9,535,161.
Addition of staff to bolster 24/7 operations at the Power Control Center and reorganize service/incidence response and enhanced reporting protocols

records.project_name: Capitalize Maintenance of Way Work Budget Alignment
records.description: Assessment of scope, criteria, and complexity of long term and larger projects related to MofW State of Good Repair (SOGR); to optimize the balance and appropriate categorization between operating budgets and capital program expenditures
Comprehensive review of maintenance, materials and non-ops service contracts for targeted reductions and changes to business practices

Material Spend Cost Reduction. Comprehensive assessment and documentation of Railroad and MTA Agency wide [Assets to Materials to Inventory to Procurement] lifecycle and expenditures; starting with Materials Management (MM) and OPEX/CAPEX optimization of funding types
Overtime Reduction. Drive accountability for overtime further down into sub-department level (management centers)

records.project_name: Rolling Stock Standard Work Inspection Dwell Time Reduction
records.description: Develop work standards that will optimize work and increase inspection throughput for our M7 series. Potential to extend these business improvements and savings across multiple locations across both Railroads in 2024, 2025 and forward

Rolling Stock Process Improvement and Resource Optimization. Extension of the M7 air brake to 10 years to more efficiently manage both the M7 air brake replacement and the M7 truck overhaul RCM program; optimize staffing for inspections and maintenance by utilizing new shop space at Harmon
Schedule & Resource Mgmt. Optimization. Utilize passenger loading data and technology to better match train lengths to customer demand and optimize resources

records.project_name: Construction of the Rail Control Center Annex at 806 9th Avenue, Manhattan
records.description: Construction and delivery of 30,000 SF of core-and-shell office space and 15 below-grade facility parking spaces within an affordable housing development on the site of the RCC parking lot
$573M available FFY 2022, maximum state award ~$115M
Strategy 7: Reduce Metro-North flooding affecting Hudson Line, Mott Haven Yard, Melrose/Tremont Corridor, and Harlem Line
Bundling the Hammels Wye and the South Channel Bridge projects together

Rehabilitation project in the Rockaways. President Crichlow discussed progress and thanked US Coast Guard, Schiavone Construction, MTA C&D, and divisions within the Department of Subways for their efforts.

records.project_name: License Agreement with Rosen's Café at 2 Broadway
records.description: Authorization to enter into a license agreement with Rosen's Café, LLC for food use in the lobby of 2 Broadway, New York, NY. Approximately 1,371 sq ft of retail space. Five year term. Minimum annual guaranteed rent of $24,000 for first year, with 3% annual increase, plus 10% of gross sales over $300,000 annual breakpoint.

records.project_name: Second Avenue Subway Phase II field office lease
records.description: Letter agreement for the Second Avenue Subway Phase II field office lease at 159 East 125th Street, New York, NY. Authorization to enter into a letter of agreement in support of the field office for SAS Phase II. Vendor: BDG Gotham Plaza, LLC. Amount: Up to $225,000.
Subway Co-Response Outreach Teams - New mental health co-response teams pairing NYC clinicians with MTAPD officers
Small-Business Development & Mentoring Program Consultant

Program to develop and grow emerging contractors through classes, on-the-job training and technical assistance on prime contracts with MTA Agencies, creating a larger pool of diverse qualified contractors who can compete for, and complete MTA construction projects safely, timely and within budget.

Historic agreement with TWU Local 100 to modernize the station agent role, bringing agents out of booths to perform core customer service functions at turnstiles, fare machines, and throughout stations. Training underway on enhanced customer service and OMNY equipment.
Transition from contracted cleaning and disinfecting of subway stations and subway cars to in-house cleaning workforce

Ground lease of portion of MTA parking lot at 2 Depot Plaza, Tarrytown, NY for 88-unit multi-family housing project with community artist workshops and galleries, plus improved access to Tarrytown Station and northbound platform

records.project_name: TBTA Sales Tax Revenue Bonds, Series 2023A
records.description: Issuance of $800,000,000 of Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority Sales Tax Revenue Bonds to generate new money proceeds to finance approved 2020-2024 Capital Program transit and commuter projects.

records.project_name: TBTA Level One Maintainers Training Program
records.description: 12 new Level One Maintainers commenced multi-disciplined training including safety protocols, roadway and electrical work, CDL, OSHA-30, forklift operation, firefighting, wrecker and aerial truck safety, Incident Command Structure, and administrative duties

Five-year estimated quantity miscellaneous service contract with Thales Transport & Security, Inc. for maintenance and support services for various NYC Transit signaling systems. Total estimated amount $59,235,090. Systems include Flushing line CBTC, Flushing line ATS, 1st Avenue SSI, Bergen Street SSI, Queens Boulevard line CBTC.

records.project_name: Miscellaneous Toll Collection Consultant Support Services
records.description: Three personal service contracts for toll collection consultant support services on an as-needed basis, including conceptual design, planning, evaluation of toll system upgrades, and design of toll-related infrastructure.

Proposal to lower the $100 administrative toll violation fee at major B&T facilities back down to $50 and prescribe a uniform $50 fee across all tolled bridge and tunnel facilities, including the Central Business District Tolling Program.

New system replacing the current obsolete database (UGOS) used across multiple departments to process requests for track access, enhancing cross-departmental coordination and improving asset management of equipment

records.project_name: Professional Services for Traffic and Revenue Assessments and Environmental Review of TBTA Toll-Related Actions
records.description: Two competitively solicited personal service contracts to WSP USA, Inc. and AECOM for Revenue Assessments and Environmental Review of TBTA's Toll-Related Actions on an as-needed basis

records.project_name: Transit Wireless License Expansion for Cellular in NYCT Tunnels and Wi-Fi at Above Ground NYCT Stations and Staten Island Railway
records.description: Expansion of Transit Wireless license to design, build, operate, maintain and sublicense a wireless communication connection system in underground tunnels, at above-ground stations and along the ROW, a free public and private facing Wi-Fi network for NYCT above-ground and SIR stations, and the non-exclusive right to market fiber optic cable.

records.project_name: Transportation Planning and Conceptual Design Retainer Contract Panel
records.description: Eleven competitively negotiated personal service contracts for transportation planning and conceptual design. Aggregate budget: $25,000,000. Contract term: April 1, 2021 to March 31, 2026. Firms: AECOM USA Inc., Arup and Partners PC, Gannett Fleming, HDR Inc., HNTB, NV5, Parsons Transportation Group, Sam Schwartz Engineering, STV Inc., VHB, WSP USA Inc.

records.project_name: UKG Kronos System Day-to-Day Managed Support Services
records.description: Contract to IBM to provide day-to-day maintenance and technical support services for MTA's UKG Workforce Central system and the new upgraded Kronos Pro Workforce Management system over three years with two 1-year renewal options.

Program to fill vacant retail units in the subway system with creative installations and cultural programming. Has activated 12 units in nine stations, working with over a dozen partners including the Whitney Museum of American Art and ChaShaMa

Eighth locomotive rebranded as part of Metro-North's Heritage Series. Locomotive No. 216 honors the service and sacrifice of the nation's veterans. Design by Emily Moser, developed with input from Metro-North's veteran workforce. Unveiled November 10 at North White Plains shop.

Proposed 1,200-unit all-affordable multifamily housing project in the Wakefield section of the Bronx, adjacent to MNR's Wakefield Station, including rezoning, fee parcel sale, access easement, and construction of an access drive.

records.project_name: Conveyance of property interests in the Wakefield section of the Bronx
records.description: Conveyance of property interests in the Wakefield section of the Bronx to facilitate adjacent transit-oriented development

Development of an approximately 550-unit low-income housing development adjacent to the Subject Property (Block 3273, Lot 101, Bronx, NY at Webster Avenue and East 197th Street)
Customer-facing campaign to make customers feel safe and comfortable, focused on mask compliance, cleanliness, and welcoming riders back.

Mixed-use development of the Eastern Rail Yard and Western Rail Yard of the LIRR's John D. Caemmerer West Side Yard, including 99-year ground leases, affordable housing, PS/IS school, cultural facility

records.project_name: Parking Fee Structure at the LIRR Westbury Parking Garage
records.description: LIRR seeks MTA Board approval to establish parking fees for 300 unrestricted daily parking spaces in the newly constructed Westbury Parking Garage, a 4-level ungated garage expected to open in March 2021, constructed as part of LIRR's Main Line Expansion project.

records.status: Conditional designation of Gotham Organization as selected proposer
records.description: Conditional Designation of Gotham Organization to construct and operate a Transit Oriented Development on the former commuter parking lot at the Westbury Station, Westbury, NY

records.status: In negotiations with multiple developers; selection expected by summer 2021
records.description: Transform 1.6-acre MTA-owned surface parking lot adjacent to LIRR Westbury station into new residential and retail development.
Investigation and remediation of property in the vicinity of the Town of Brookhaven Landfill (Yaphank Landfill) including acquisition of temporary and permanent easements on adjacent properties

Internal facing dispatching application that supports bus operations and bus management. Includes features such as unscheduled pullouts, historical snapshots of track sheets, visual indicators for recently assigned buses, integrated electric bus data, and displays bus status from non-depot locations.
